items.reason: Replaces old log_rate_limit guidance with the new three-rate LogSampler behavior, derivation rules, and configuration examples.
Removes DATASYSTEM_LOG_RATE_LIMIT after clients stop independently configuring sampling via environment variables.
Replaces the dscli log_rate_limit option with request_sample_rate, access_sample_rate, diagnostic_sample_rate, and derivation semantics.
Adds routing entries for the new log sampler design context.
Updates the logging module overview to include LogSampler responsibilities and route readers to the new design doc.
Adds a detailed source-backed design for the new random request/access/diagnostic sampling behavior and performance constraints.
The Helm values now expose requestSampleRate/accessSampleRate/diagnosticSampleRate and remove logRateLimit, but the K8s configuration reference was not updated.
This logging design doc still describes log_rate_limit, DATASYSTEM_LOG_RATE_LIMIT, LogRateLimiter, and client sync behavior that the PR replaces.
This trace context doc still says SetRequestTraceUUID and RPC import decisions depend on local log_rate_limit and LogRateLimiter.
The PLOG slow-log plan still frames bypass behavior around log_rate_limit/LogRateLimiter and does not reflect the new diagnostic_sample_rate model.
The PLOG slow-get summary still describes bypassing log_rate_limit request sampling rather than the new request/diagnostic sampling behavior.
